notes:
Chào bác means “hello” to someone older than your parents (about your parents’ age or older). It’s polite and respectful.

Bác is a kinship term for your parent’s older sibling, but in daily life it’s also used for older adults. Vietnamese uses family terms instead of “you,” so picking the right word (bác, cô, chú…) matters.

To sound natural, say it clearly and slightly formally—this is respectful speech, not casual friend talk.
exampleSentences: Mỗi sáng, em đều chào bác hàng xóm khi đi học.
Anh nhớ chào bác khi đến nhà họ chơi cùng bạn bè.
Chị dặn em phải luôn chào bác khi gặp ngoài đường.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Every morning, I greet elder neighbor when I go to school.
Remember to greet elder when you visit their house with friends.
She told me to always greet elder when meeting on the street.

To sound natural, say it clearly and slightly formally—this is respectful speech, not casual friend talk.
